verses.latin_vulgate: Et cum egrederetur de templo, ait illi unus ex discipulis suis: Magister, aspice quales lapides, et quales structuræ.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐκπορευομένου αὐτοῦ ἐκ τοῦ ἱεροῦ λέγει αὐτῷ εἷς τῶν μαθητῶν αὐτοῦ διδάσκαλε ἴδε ποταποὶ λίθοι καὶ ποταπαὶ οἰκοδομαί
verses.king_james: And as he went out of the temple, one of his disciples saith unto him, Master, see what manner of stones and what buildings *are here*!

verses.latin_vulgate: Et respondens Jesus, ait illi: Vides has omnes magnas ædificationes? Non relinquetur lapis super lapidem, qui non destruatur.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ὁ ἰησοῦς ἀποκριθεὶς εἶπεν αὐτῷ βλέπεις ταύτας τὰς μεγάλας οἰκοδομάς οὐ μὴ ἀφεθῇ λίθος ἐπὶ λίθῷ, ὃς οὐ μὴ καταλυθῇ
verses.king_james: And Jesus answering said unto him, Seest thou these great buildings? there shall not be left one stone upon another, that shall not be thrown down.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et cum sederet in monte Olivarum contra templum, interrogabant eum separatim Petrus, et Jacobus, et Joannes, et Andreas:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ καθημένου αὐτοῦ εἰς τὸ ὄρος τῶν ἐλαιῶν κατέναντι τοῦ ἱεροῦ ἐπηρώτων αὐτὸν κατ᾽ ἰδίαν πέτρος καὶ ἰάκωβος καὶ ἰωάννης καὶ ἀνδρέας
verses.king_james: And as he sat upon the mount of Olives over against the temple, Peter and James and John and Andrew asked him privately,

verses.latin_vulgate: Dic nobis, quando ista fient? et quod signum erit, quando hæc omnia incipient consummari?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: εἰπὲ ἡμῖν πότε ταῦτα ἔσται καὶ τί τὸ σημεῖον ὅταν μέλλῃ πάντα ταῦτα συντελεῖσθαι
verses.king_james: Tell us, when shall these things be? and what *shall be* the sign when all these things shall be fulfilled?

verses.latin_vulgate: Et respondens Jesus cœpit dicere illis: Videte ne quid vos seducat:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ δὲ ἰησοῦς ἀποκριθεὶς αὐτοῖς ἤρξατο λέγειν βλέπετε μή τις ὑμᾶς πλανήσῃ
verses.king_james: And Jesus answering them began to say, Take heed lest any *man* deceive you:

verses.latin_vulgate: multi enim venient in nomine meo, dicentes quia ego sum: et multos seducent.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πολλοὶ γὰρ ἐλεύσονται ἐπὶ τῷ ὀνόματί μου λέγοντες ὅτι ἐγώ εἰμι καὶ πολλοὺς πλανήσουσιν
verses.king_james: For many shall come in my name, saying, I am *Christ*; and shall deceive many.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cum audieritis autem bella, et opiniones bellorum, ne timueritis: oportet enim hæc fieri: sed nondum finis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὅταν δὲ ἀκούσητε πολέμους καὶ ἀκοὰς πολέμων μὴ θροεῖσθε δεῖ γὰρ γενέσθαι ἀλλ᾽ οὔπω τὸ τέλος
verses.king_james: And when ye shall hear of wars and rumours of wars, be ye not troubled: for *such things* must needs be; but the end *shall* not *be* yet.

verses.latin_vulgate: Exsurget enim gens contra gentem, et regnum super regnum, et erunt terræmotus per loca, et fames. Initium dolorum hæc.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐγερθήσεται γὰρ ἔθνος ἐπὶ ἔθνος καὶ βασιλεία ἐπὶ βασιλείαν καὶ ἔσονται σεισμοὶ κατὰ τόπους καὶ ἔσονται λιμοὶ καὶ ταραχαί ἀρχαὶ ὠδίνων ταῦτα
verses.king_james: For nation shall rise against nation, and kingdom against kingdom: and there shall be earthquakes in divers places, and there shall be famines and troubles: these *are* the beginnings of sorrows.

verses.latin_vulgate: Videte autem vosmetipsos. Tradent enim vos in consiliis, et in synagogis vapulabitis, et ante præsides et reges stabitis propter me, in testimonium illis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: βλέπετε δὲ ὑμεῖς ἑαυτούς παραδώσουσιν γὰρ ὑμᾶς εἰς συνέδρια καὶ εἰς συναγωγὰς δαρήσεσθε καὶ ἐπὶ ἡγεμόνων καὶ βασιλέων σταθήσεσθε ἕνεκεν ἐμοῦ εἰς μαρτύριον αὐτοῖς
verses.king_james: But take heed to yourselves: for they shall deliver you up to councils; and in the synagogues ye shall be beaten: and ye shall be brought before rulers and kings for my sake, for a testimony against them.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et in omnes gentes primum oportet prædicari Evangelium.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ εἰς πάντα τὰ ἔθνη δεῖ πρῶτον κηρυχθῆναι τὸ εὐαγγέλιον
verses.king_james: And the gospel must first be published among all nations.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et cum duxerint vos tradentes, nolite præcogitare quid loquamini: sed quod datum vobis fuerit in illa hora, id loquimini: non enim vos estis loquentes, sed Spiritus Sanctus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὅταν δὲ ἄγαγωσιν ὑμᾶς παραδιδόντες μὴ προμεριμνᾶτε τί λαλήσητε μηδὲ μελετᾶτε ἀλλ᾽ ὃ ἐὰν δοθῇ ὑμῖν ἐν ἐκείνῃ τῇ ὥρᾳ τοῦτο λαλεῖτε οὐ γάρ ἐστε ὑμεῖς οἱ λαλοῦντες ἀλλὰ τὸ πνεῦμα τὸ ἅγιον
verses.king_james: But when they shall lead *you*, and deliver you up, take no thought beforehand what ye shall speak, neither do ye premeditate: but whatsoever shall be given you in that hour, that speak ye: for it is not ye that speak, but the Holy Ghost.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tradet autem frater fratrem in mortem, et pater filium: et consurgent filii in parentes, et morte afficient eos.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: παραδώσει δὲ ἀδελφὸς ἀδελφὸν εἰς θάνατον καὶ πατὴρ τέκνον καὶ ἐπαναστήσονται τέκνα ἐπὶ γονεῖς καὶ θανατώσουσιν αὐτούς
verses.king_james: Now the brother shall betray the brother to death, and the father the son; and children shall rise up against *their* parents, and shall cause them to be put to death.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et eritis odio omnibus propter nomen meum. Qui autem sustinuerit in finem, hic salvus erit.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἔσεσθε μισούμενοι ὑπὸ πάντων διὰ τὸ ὄνομά μου ὁ δὲ ὑπομείνας εἰς τέλος οὗτος σωθήσεται
verses.king_james: And ye shall be hated of all *men* for my name's sake: but he that shall endure unto the end, the same shall be saved.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cum autem videritis abominationem desolationis stantem, ubi non debet, qui legit, intelligat: tunc qui in Judæa sunt, fugiant in montes:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὅταν δὲ ἴδητε τὸ βδέλυγμα τῆς ἐρημώσεως τὸ ῥηθὲν ὑπὸ δανιὴλ τοῦ προφήτου, ἑστός ὅπου οὐ δεῖ ὁ ἀναγινώσκων νοείτω τότε οἱ ἐν τῇ ἰουδαίᾳ φευγέτωσαν εἰς τὰ ὄρη
verses.king_james: But when ye shall see the abomination of desolation, spoken of by Daniel the prophet, standing where it ought not, (let him that readeth understand,) then let them that be in Judaea flee to the mountains:

verses.latin_vulgate: et qui super tectum, ne descendat in domum, nec introëat ut tollat quid de domo sua:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ δὲ ἐπὶ τοῦ δώματος μὴ καταβάτω εἰς τὴν οἰκίαν, μηδὲ εἰσελθέτω ἆραί τι ἐκ τῆς οἰκίας αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: And let him that is on the housetop not go down into the house, neither enter *therein*, to take any thing out of his house:

verses.latin_vulgate: et qui in agro erit, non revertatur retro tollere vestimentum suum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ὁ εἰς τὸν ἀγρὸν ὢν μὴ ἐπιστρεψάτω εἰς τὰ ὀπίσω ἆραι τὸ ἱμάτιον αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: And let him that is in the field not turn back again for to take up his garment.

verses.latin_vulgate: Væ autem prægnantibus et nutrientibus in illis diebus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὐαὶ δὲ ταῖς ἐν γαστρὶ ἐχούσαις καὶ ταῖς θηλαζούσαις ἐν ἐκείναις ταῖς ἡμέραις
verses.king_james: But woe to them that are with child, and to them that give suck in those days!

verses.latin_vulgate: Orate vero ut hieme non fiant.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: προσεύχεσθε δὲ ἵνα μὴ γένηται ἠ φυγὴ ὑμῶν χειμῶνος
verses.king_james: And pray ye that your flight be not in the winter.

verses.latin_vulgate: Erunt enim dies illi tribulationes tales quales non fuerunt ab initio creaturæ, quam condidit Deus usque nunc, neque fient.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἔσονται γὰρ αἱ ἡμέραι ἐκεῖναι θλῖψις οἵα οὐ γέγονεν τοιαύτη ἀπ᾽ ἀρχῆς κτίσεως ἣς ἔκτισεν ὁ θεὸς ἕως τοῦ νῦν καὶ οὐ μὴ γένηται
verses.king_james: For *in* those days shall be affliction, such as was not from the beginning of the creation which God created unto this time, neither shall be.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et nisi breviasset Dominus dies, non fuisset salva omnis caro: sed propter electos, quos elegit, breviavit dies.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ εἰ μὴ κύριος ἐκολόβωσεν τὰς ἡμέρας οὐκ ἂν ἐσώθη πᾶσα σάρξ ἀλλὰ διὰ τοὺς ἐκλεκτοὺς οὓς ἐξελέξατο ἐκολόβωσεν τὰς ἡμέρας
verses.king_james: And except that the Lord had shortened those days, no flesh should be saved: but for the elect's sake, whom he hath chosen, he hath shortened the days.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et tunc si quis vobis dixerit: Ecce hic est Christus, ecce illic, ne credideritis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ τότε ἐάν τις ὑμῖν εἴπῃ Ἴδοὺ ὧδε ὁ χριστός ἢ ἰδού, ἐκεῖ μὴ πιστεύσητε
verses.king_james: And then if any man shall say to you, Lo, here *is* Christ; or, lo, *he is* there; believe *him* not:

verses.latin_vulgate: Exsurgent enim pseudochristi et pseudoprophetæ, et dabunt signa et portenta ad seducendos, si fieri potest, etiam electos.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐγερθήσονται γὰρ ψευδόχριστοι καὶ ψευδοπροφῆται καὶ δώσουσιν σημεῖα καὶ τέρατα πρὸς τὸ ἀποπλανᾶν εἰ δυνατόν καὶ τοὺς ἐκλεκτούς
verses.king_james: For false Christs and false prophets shall rise, and shall shew signs and wonders, to seduce, if *it were* possible, even the elect.

verses.latin_vulgate: Vos ergo videte: ecce prædixi vobis omnia.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὑμεῖς δὲ βλέπετε ἰδοῦ προείρηκα ὑμῖν πάντα
verses.king_james: But take ye heed: behold, I have foretold you all things.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sed in illis diebus, post tribulationem illam, sol contenebrabitur, et luna non dabit splendorem suum:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀλλ᾽ ἐν ἐκείναις ταῖς ἡμέραις μετὰ τὴν θλῖψιν ἐκείνην ὁ ἥλιος σκοτισθήσεται καὶ ἡ σελήνη οὐ δώσει τὸ φέγγος αὐτῆς
verses.king_james: But in those days, after that tribulation, the sun shall be darkened, and the moon shall not give her light,

verses.latin_vulgate: et stellæ cæli erunt decidentes, et virtutes, quæ in cælis sunt, movebuntur.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ οἱ ἀστέρες τοῦ οὐρανοῦ ἔσονται ἐκπίπτοντες, καὶ αἱ δυνάμεις αἱ ἐν τοῖς οὐρανοῖς σαλευθήσονται
verses.king_james: And the stars of heaven shall fall, and the powers that are in heaven shall be shaken.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et tunc videbunt Filium hominis venientem in nubibus cum virtute multa et gloria.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ τότε ὄψονται τὸν υἱὸν τοῦ ἀνθρώπου ἐρχόμενον ἐν νεφέλαις μετὰ δυνάμεως πολλῆς καὶ δόξης
verses.king_james: And then shall they see the Son of man coming in the clouds with great power and glory.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et tunc mittet angelos suos, et congregabit electos suos a quatuor ventis, a summo terræ usque ad summum cæli.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ τότε ἀποστελεῖ τοὺς ἀγγέλους αὐτοῦ καὶ ἐπισυνάξει τοὺς ἐκλεκτοὺς αὐτοῦ, ἐκ τῶν τεσσάρων ἀνέμων ἀπ᾽ ἄκρου γῆς ἕως ἄκρου οὐρανοῦ
verses.king_james: And then shall he send his angels, and shall gather together his elect from the four winds, from the uttermost part of the earth to the uttermost part of heaven.

verses.latin_vulgate: A ficu autem discite parabolam. Cum jam ramus ejus tener fuerit, et nata fuerint folia, cognoscitis quia in proximo sit æstas:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀπὸ δὲ τῆς συκῆς μάθετε τὴν παραβολήν ὅταν αὐτῆς ἤδη ὁ κλάδος ἁπαλὸς γένηται καὶ ἐκφύῃ τὰ φύλλα γινώσκετε ὅτι ἐγγὺς τὸ θέρος ἐστίν
verses.king_james: Now learn a parable of the fig tree; When her branch is yet tender, and putteth forth leaves, ye know that summer is near:

verses.latin_vulgate: sic et vos cum videritis hæc fieri, scitote quod in proximo sit, in ostiis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὕτως καὶ ὑμεῖς ὅταν ταῦτα ἴδητε γινόμενα γινώσκετε ὅτι ἐγγύς ἐστιν ἐπὶ θύραις
verses.king_james: So ye in like manner, when ye shall see these things come to pass, know that it is nigh, *even* at the doors.

verses.latin_vulgate: Amen dico vobis, quoniam non transibit generatio hæc, donec omnia ista fiant.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀμὴν λέγω ὑμῖν ὅτι οὐ μὴ παρέλθῃ ἡ γενεὰ αὕτη μέχρις οὗ πάντα ταῦτα γένηται
verses.king_james: Verily I say unto you, that this generation shall not pass, till all these things be done.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cælum et terra transibunt, verba autem mea non transibunt.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ οὐρανὸς καὶ ἡ γῆ παρελεύσονται οἱ δὲ λόγοι μου οὐ μὴ παρέλθωσιν
verses.king_james: Heaven and earth shall pass away: but my words shall not pass away.

verses.latin_vulgate: De die autem illo vel hora nemo scit, neque angeli in cælo, neque Filius, nisi Pater.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: περὶ δὲ τῆς ἡμέρας ἐκείνης καὶ τῆς ὥρας οὐδεὶς οἶδεν οὐδὲ οἱ ἄγγελοι οἱ ἐν οὐρανῷ οὐδὲ ὁ υἱός εἰ μὴ ὁ πατήρ
verses.king_james: But of that day and *that* hour knoweth no man, no, not the angels which are in heaven, neither the Son, but the Father.

verses.latin_vulgate: Videte, vigilate, et orate: nescitis enim quando tempus sit.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: βλέπετε ἀγρυπνεῖτε καὶ προσεύχεσθε οὐκ οἴδατε γὰρ πότε ὁ καιρός ἐστιν
verses.king_james: Take ye heed, watch and pray: for ye know not when the time is.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sicut homo qui peregre profectus reliquit domum suam, et dedit servis suis potestatem cujusque operis, et janitori præcepit ut vigilet,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὡς ἄνθρωπος ἀπόδημος ἀφεὶς τὴν οἰκίαν αὐτοῦ καὶ δοὺς τοῖς δούλοις αὐτοῦ τὴν ἐξουσίαν καὶ ἑκάστῳ τὸ ἔργον αὐτοῦ καὶ τῷ θυρωρῷ ἐνετείλατο ἵνα γρηγορῇ
verses.king_james: For the Son of man is as a man taking a far journey, who left his house, and gave authority to his servants, and to every man his work, and commanded the porter to watch.

verses.latin_vulgate: vigilate ergo (nescitis enim quando dominus domus veniat: sero, an media nocte, an galli cantu, an mane),
verses.greek_textus_receptus: γρηγορεῖτε οὖν οὐκ οἴδατε γὰρ πότε ὁ κύριος τῆς οἰκίας ἔρχεται ὀψὲ ἢ μεσονυκτίου, ἢ ἀλεκτοροφωνίας ἢ πρωΐ
verses.king_james: Watch ye therefore: for ye know not when the master of the house cometh, at even, or at midnight, or at the cockcrowing, or in the morning:

verses.latin_vulgate: ne, cum venerit repente, inveniat vos dormientes.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μὴ ἐλθὼν ἐξαίφνης εὕρῃ ὑμᾶς καθεύδοντας
verses.king_james: Lest coming suddenly he find you sleeping.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quod autem vobis dico, omnibus dico: Vigilate.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἃ δὲ ὑμῖν λέγω πᾶσιν λέγω γρηγορεῖτε
verses.king_james: And what I say unto you I say unto all, Watch.
